title: Glossary for 1 language pair (terms, proper nouns, transliteration, do-not-translate)
context: Museum heritage translation is error-prone on proper nouns, period terms, material names, and culture-specific vocabulary. A controlled glossary for each language pair ensures that terminology is consistent across descriptions and reviewable by bilingual reviewers. The glossary must also specify proper-noun conventions (preserve vs. transliterate), transliteration rules, and a do-not-translate list to prevent the most common translation errors. The glossary schema (objectSchema/glossarySchema from schema-001) must be in place before this task.
objective: Build an initial glossary YAML for 1 language pair (source language → first target language) with ≥40 entries, covering: heritage/art-history terminology (object types, materials, techniques, periods); proper nouns (artist names, place names, period names) with preserve/transliterate conventions; a transliteration table where applicable; and a do-not-translate list (proper nouns and specialised terms to preserve verbatim in the target language).
acceptanceCriteria: YAML file validated against glossarySchema from schema-001; contains ≥40 entries
Each entry contains: term (source language), translation or preserve directive, part-of-speech, and a notes field for context or usage examples

Separate sections for: general heritage/art-history terminology, proper nouns (artist names, place names, period names) with preserve/transliterate annotation, transliteration conventions (if applicable for the script pair), and do-not-translate list
Reviewed and signed off by a qualified bilingual reviewer (native or near-native target language competence with heritage-translation experience)
Glossary version and reviewer sign-off recorded in the file header
output: glossaries/<src>-<tgt>.yaml — initial glossary for the first language pair, validated against glossarySchema
